LAW AND MOTION 301, DEFENDANT CREDIT SUISSE FIRST BOSTON CORPORATION'S PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION -- PETITION TO COMPEL ARBITRATION IS GRANTED; PLAINTIFF IS DIRECTED TO FILE HIS CLAIM WITH THE NASD. IF THE NASD REFUSES TO ACCEPT THE CLAIM WITHOUT THE WAIVER, PLAINTIFF CAN FILE A MOTION TO RESTORE ACTION TO THE CIVIL ACTIVE LIST. IN THE MEANTIME, THE ACTION WILL BE STAYED PENDING THE OUTCOME OF THE ARBITRATION. JUDGE: JAMES L. WARREN REPORTER: CHRISTINA PAXTON, CSR# 1558

LAW AND MOTION 301, AMENDED DEMURRER. DEFENDANT FRANK QUATTRONE'S AMENDED DEMURRER -- DEMURRER SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND TO PLEAD FACTS RE: STATUTE OF LIMITATIONS TOLLING AGREEMENTS AS TO DEFENDANTQUATTRONE; I.E. WHO, WHAT WHEN, WHERE & HOW WERE REPRESENTATIONS MADE INDICATINGDEFENDANT QUATTRONE WAS REPRESENTED IN TOLLING AGREEMENTS. PLAINTIFF IS ALLOWED TO PLEAD IN C/A 6-9 RE: INDIVIDUAL WRONGDOING OF DEFENDANT AND WHY HIS CONDUCT IS OUTSIDE HIS SCOPE OF EMPLOYMENT, TO PLEAD AROUND STATUTE OF LIMITATION RE: WHY THIS WILL EXHAUST REMEDY. AS TO 10TH, 11TH & 12TH C/A, DEMURRER IS SUSTAINED WITHOUT LEAVE TO AMEND AS TO DEFENDANT QUATTRONE. DEMURRER OTHERWISE OVERRULED. PLAINTIFF HAS 10 DAYS LEAVE TO AMEND TO CONFORM COMPLAINT TO THIS RULING. (DAG/301/DR) REPORTER: JOSEPH VICKSTEIN, CSR 4780

Notice Of Hearing On Petition And Motion; Petition Of Deft. To Compel Arbitraion; Notice Of Dodging Federal Authorities In Support
Off Calendar Nov-19-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
NO APPEARANCES ON THIS MATTER. THE COURT ADOPTED ITS TENTATIVE RULING: DEFENDANT CREDIT SUISSE FIRST BOSTON CORPORATION'S Petition To Compel Arbitration -- Petition to compel arbitration is granted; Plaintiff is directed to file his claim with the NASD. If the NASD refuses to accept the claim without the waiver, Plaintiff can file a motion to restore action to the civil active list. In the meantime, the action will be stayed pending the outcome of the arbitration. (301/JLW/KM-X) Reporter: Christina Paxton, CSR #1558

AMENDED DEMURRER
Off Calendar Feb-10-2003 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:
APPEARANCES:
Kenneth G. Hausman, Esq. of HOWARD RICE 434-1600, for defendant Frank Quattrone, demurring party
Amanda McClintock, Esq. of GIRARDI & KEESE 213/977-0211, for plaintiff and opposing party

RULING:
A R G U E D and the Court adopted its tentative ruling as modified -- DEFENDANT
FRANK QUATTRONE's AMENDED DEMURRER -- Demurrer sustained with leave to
amend to plead facts re: statute of limitations tolling agreements as to defendant
Quattrone; i.e. who, what when, where & how were representations made indicating
defendant Quattrone was represented in tolling agreements. plaintiff is allowed to
plead in c/a 6-9 re: individual wrongdoing of defendant and why his conduct is
outside his scope of employment, to plead around statute of limitation re: why this
will exhaust remedy.
As to 10th, 11th & 12th c/a, demurrer is sustained without leave to amend as to
defendant Quattrone.
Demurrer otherwise overruled. Plaintiff has 10 days leave to amend to conform
complaint to this ruling. (DAG/301/DR) Reporter: Joseph Vickstein, CSR 4780
